To solve the problem, we will proceed step by step, beginning with the surface area calculation using the given parametric equations and then moving on to the total electrical conductivity.

---

### Step 1: Surface Area Calculation

The surface area $ S $ of a parametric surface defined by $ \mathbf{r}(u, v) = (x(u, v), y(u, v), z(u, v)) $ is given by the double integral:

$$
S = \iint_D \left| \frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial v} \right| \, du \, dv
$$

Given:

$$
x(u, v) = a \sin(u) \cos(v)
$$
$$
y(u, v) = a \sin(u) \sin(v)
$$
$$
z(u, v) = b \cos(u)
$$

We compute the partial derivatives:

$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial u} = \left( a \cos(u) \cos(v), a \cos(u) \sin(v), -b \sin(u) \right)
$$
$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial v} = \left( -a \sin(u) \sin(v), a \sin(u) \cos(v), 0 \right)
$$

Now compute the cross product:

$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial v} =
\begin{vmatrix}
\mathbf{i} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\
a \cos(u) \cos(v) & a \cos(u) \sin(v) & -b \sin(u) \\
-a \sin(u) \sin(v) & a \sin(u) \cos(v) & 0
\end{vmatrix}
$$

Expanding the determinant:

- \mathbf{j} \left( a \cos(u) \cos(v) \cdot 0 - (-b \sin(u)) \cdot (-a \sin(u) \sin
